_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize 5 Alaska House members: sara-hannan (HD4 D), louise-stutes (HD5 R-in-coalition, running for STATE SENATE not House reelection), sarah-vance (HD6 R, strong social conservative), justin-ruffridge (HD7 R, pharmacist - HB195 pro-life deviation), bill-elam (HD8 R, first-term, HB195 deviation). Every TRUE/FALSE cites a URL; null where no evidence; never inferred from party. Cross-party deviations flagged: Hannan red-flag cosponsor; Stutes flipped to kill pro-life amendment + voted anti-trans-care; Ruffridge/Elam (Rs) voted YES on HB195 abortion-pill expansion.

profile.background: Alaska House District 4 (downtown Juneau + Douglas Island; formerly District 33 pre-redistricting), Democrat, in office since Jan 2019; sits on House Finance. 21-year public high-school teacher. Running UNOPPOSED for reelection in 2026 (filed by the June 2 2026 deadline).
profile.confidence_note: Documented progressive across life, LGBTQ policy, and guns - scored only on cited votes/cosponsorships/statements; everything else null. A reported 2024 Planned Parenthood endorsement could NOT be confirmed against a primary source (PP pages are JS-rendered) so it is left null rather than scored.

sara-hannan.notes_set: Evidence finalization 2026-06-17. Roster VERIFIED (ADN 6/3/2026: 'Rep. Sara Hannan, a Juneau Democrat... represents District 4'); running unopposed 2026. HARD progressive: opposed defunding Medicaid abortion services, calling abortion 'fundamental health care for women' (2022); named co-sponsor of HB 301 (2026) adding sexual-orientation/gender-identity-or-expression anti-discrimination protections; named PRIME co-sponsor of HB 89 (2025) 'Gun Violence Protective Orders' red-flag law. NO cross-party deviation found - every documented position is standard progressive. CORRECTION to prior premise: Hannan is NOT a sponsor of HB 195 (the Juneau co-sponsor is Andi Story, HD3); the House passed HB 195 by unanimous consent so no individual Hannan roll call exists - not scored. NO EVIDENCE on marriage definition, education choice, religious liberty, economics, elections/RCV, crime, federalism, or immigration.

0.note: Opposed defunding Medicaid abortion services and called abortion 'fundamental health care for women' (2022) - contrary to affirming personhood/protection of life from conception.

1.note: Defends public abortion access - the opposite of advocating abortion abolition.

2.note: Named co-sponsor of HB 301 (2026) creating gender-identity/expression anti-discrimination protections - contrary to rejecting transgender ideology / affirming immutable biological sex in law.

4.note: Co-sponsored SOGI anti-discrimination legislation - contrary to opposing promotion of LGBTQ+ identity in public policy.

1.note: Named PRIME co-sponsor of HB 89 (2025) red-flag 'Gun Violence Protective Orders' law - contrary to opposing red-flag laws and gun restrictions.

profile.background: Alaska House District 5 (Kodiak Island), registered Republican but a 'Majority Coalition Republican' who has caucused with the bipartisan/Democrat-led House Majority Coalition since 2017; Speaker of the House 2021-2023 (elected by the Democratic coalition); current Chair of House Rules and longtime fisheries/Marine Highway lead. In office since 2015.
profile.confidence_note: CANDIDACY CORRECTION: Stutes is NOT running for House reelection in 2026 - she announced (May 2025) a run for the ALASKA STATE SENATE seat being vacated by retiring Senate President Gary Stevens (R). candidacy_status set to running_higher_office; office kept as her sitting House seat. A registered R who governs inside the Democratic-led coalition: she breaks left on abortion/education and right on transgender care. Small denominator; HB 195 individual roll call could not be machine-verified and is left unscored.

louise-stutes.notes_set: Evidence finalization 2026-06-17. Roster VERIFIED + CANDIDACY CORRECTED: Louise Stutes (R, HD5 Kodiak) is running for the STATE SENATE in 2026 (Gary Stevens's open seat), NOT for House reelection (Alaska Public Media 5/22/2025). DEVIATIONS documented in one 4/14-16/2025 budget session: (1) LEFT - she initially voted FOR the 25-year-standing anti-abortion Medicaid budget amendment then CHANGED her vote before voting closed, killing it 20-21; stated 'the state doesn't belong between a woman and her doctor - and our state constitution protects that' (Alaska Beacon 4/16/2025) - scored FALSE on life. (2) RIGHT - hours later she joined the 19-member Republican minority to ADD budget intent language barring state funding of gender-dysphoria/transgender care - scored TRUE on the transgender cell. Strong PRO-public-school-funding record: voted YES on HB 57 (+$700 BSA) and to OVERRIDE Gov. Dunleavy's education vetoes (5/2025, 8/2025), against the Republican governor; no school-choice support found - routine public-school funding, not scored as a choice position. HB 195 (2026) individual vote NOT verifiable (passed 32-8; akleg roll-call PDFs JS-blocked) - left null. NO Planned Parenthood endorsement found. NO EVIDENCE on religious liberty, economics/CBDC, elections/RCV, crime, federalism, immigration, or guns.

0.note: Changed her vote at the last moment to defeat (20-21) the long-standing anti-abortion Medicaid budget amendment and stated 'the state doesn't belong between a woman and her doctor' - contrary to affirming protection of life from conception.

1.note: Her decisive vote killed the pro-life funding restriction - the opposite of advocating abortion abolition.

2.note: Joined the Republican minority to ADD FY budget intent language barring state funding of gender-dysphoria/transgender care (4/2025) - affirms biological sex / rejects state-funded gender transition. Cross-party-direction credit where earned.

profile.background: Alaska House District 6 (Homer/southern Kenai Peninsula), Republican, in office since 2019; Chair of House Judiciary (33rd Leg.). Among the chamber's most outspoken social conservatives. Won 2024 reelection 52.3% via RCV. iVoterGuide 'Conservative' (60/100); John Birch Society Freedom Index cumulative 70%.
profile.confidence_note: Strong, well-documented social-conservative record scored from sponsored bills, recorded votes, and a cited iVoterGuide candidate questionnaire. 2026 reelection filing not yet formally confirmed (campaign uses 'Re-elect' language; Ballotpedia showed no official 2026 candidates at research date) - candidacy_status left 'incumbent'. Held credit cells to a high bar; HB 195 individual roll call (akleg PDFs JS-blocked) not verified, so not scored - but her pro-life sponsorship record is independently documented.

sarah-vance.notes_set: Evidence finalization 2026-06-17. Roster VERIFIED (Ballotpedia/akleg HD6, R). HARD conservative across the board: PRIME sponsor of HB 302 'Heartbeat' bill (2020) and HB 357 'Alaska Heartbeat Act' (2026), and sponsor of the FY26 Medicaid-abortion-defunding amendment (voted YES, failed 20-21); iVoterGuide 'preserving life from conception to grave.' Marriage: 'Marriage is a God-ordained...union of one man and one woman' and sponsored HB 183 (2024) restricting transgender participation in girls' sports (passed House 21-9 over a minority filibuster). Education: co-sponsor of HJR 28 (constitutional amendment to allow public funds to private/religious schools after the Alexander correspondence-allotment ruling) and 'strongly supports school choice.' Religious liberty: opposes compelling businesses to violate religious beliefs; 'Churches are essential and should not be closed.' Elections: PRIME sponsor of HB 4 to repeal ranked-choice voting/open primaries, criticizing computer-algorithm counting vs. people simply counting votes. Federalism: co-sponsor of HB 175 (2021) barring COVID-19 vaccine mandates. Border: 'strongly agrees' the US should physically secure the southern border. Guns: 'I support the Second Amendment. Period.' and opposes suing gun manufacturers. NO EVIDENCE of squish on any social issue; crime-bill self-claims (HB 66) left unscored for lack of an independent cite; CBDC/ESG/balanced-budget specifics null.

0.note: Prime sponsor of two fetal-heartbeat bills (HB 302 2020, HB 357 2026); stated she believes in 'preserving life from conception to grave' - affirms personhood from conception.
Stated 'Marriage is a God-ordained, sacred and legal union of one man and one woman.'

2.note: Sponsored and voted for HB 183 (2024) restricting transgender participation in girls' sports; 'strongly disagrees' that gender identity should be a protected class - affirms immutable biological sex.

4.note: Sponsored HB 183 limiting transgender-identity participation in public-school sports - opposes promotion of LGBTQ+ identity in schools/policy.

0.note: Co-sponsor of HJR 28 (constitutional amendment to permit public funds for private/religious/correspondence schools) and 'strongly supports school choice.'
Stated 'Churches are essential and should not be closed' and grounds her public advocacy in Christian faith - affirms the right to publicly profess Christ in the public square.

1.note: 'Strongly disagrees' that businesses must provide services that violate their religious beliefs - supports conscience exemptions for Christian business owners.

0.note: Prime sponsor of HB 4 to repeal ranked-choice voting; criticized votes being 'counted by the use of a computer, using an algorithm instead of people being able to simply count and tally a vote' - favors human/paper counting over electronic tabulation.

1.note: Co-sponsor of HB 175 (2021) barring public/private COVID-19 vaccine mandates as a condition of employment or access to public services - declined federally-driven mandate overreach.

0.note: 'Strongly agrees' the US should do more to physically secure the southern border.

1.note: 'I support the Second Amendment. Period.' and 'strongly disagrees' that victims should be able to sue gun manufacturers - opposes new firearm restrictions/liability.

profile.background: Alaska House District 7 (Kenai/Soldotna), Republican, in office since Jan 2023; a pharmacist (PharmD; former chair of the Alaska Board of Pharmacy). Co-chaired House Education in the 33rd Legislature; a habitual cross-party voter (bipartisan 'Future Caucus'). Confirmed running for reelection in 2026 (primary Aug 18; challengers Daniel Cooper R, Kate Veh D).
profile.confidence_note: Headline catch is a documented PRO-LIFE DEVIATION on HB 195 (2026), the pharmacist-prescribing/abortion-pill bill, all in the official House Journal of 5/15/2026. He is NOT the bill's sponsor (sponsors: Mina-D, Gray-D, Prax-R, Story-D) - correcting a common premise - but his recorded votes are the substance. Small denominator; most other issue areas had no findable position and are null.

justin-ruffridge.notes_set: Evidence finalization 2026-06-17. Roster VERIFIED (akleg HD7, R; KDLL 6/11/2026 confirms 2026 reelection bid). HB 195 PRO-LIFE DEVIATION (House Journal 5/15/2026, pp.2614-2616): as the chamber's pharmacist Ruffridge signed the House HSS Committee 'Do Pass' on HB 195, then on the floor voted YEA to TABLE (kill, 23-17) Rep. Allard's Amendment No. 1 that would have explicitly barred pharmacists from dispensing 'an abortion-inducing drug,' and voted YEA on final passage (32-8); he twice moved to abstain citing a conflict of interest but was required to vote and voted to advance the bill. Pro-life groups (Alaska Right to Life, National Right to Life, Alaska Watchman) scored HB 195 as expanding chemical-abortion infrastructure - scored FALSE on life. COUNTER (conservative direction): in the 33rd Legislature he voted in House Education to ADVANCE the transgender-girls'-sports-restriction bill (HB 105, 2024), though he 'declined to comment' - scored TRUE on the transgender cell as a recorded vote to move a conservative bill. Education: supported the original House HB 69 BSA bill but voted to SUSTAIN the governor's veto of the stripped Senate version, then voted YES to OVERRIDE the HB 57 veto (8/2025) - fiscal-pragmatic education votes that do not map to a rubric penalty cell, noted only; his HB 69 created a charter-school coordinator (thin for 'universal school choice' - null). NO EVIDENCE on marriage definition, religious liberty, economics, elections/RCV, crime, federalism, immigration, or guns.

0.note: Voted YEA on final passage of HB 195 (32-8), which National Right to Life and Alaska Right to Life scored as expanding chemical/abortion-pill access - contrary to affirming protection of life from conception.

1.note: Voted YEA to TABLE (kill, 23-17) Rep. Allard's amendment that would have barred pharmacists from dispensing an abortion-inducing drug - voted against a pro-life restriction, the opposite of advocating abolition.

2.note: Voted in House Education Committee (2024) to advance a bill expanding the transgender-girls'-sports restriction - a recorded vote to move a conservative bill (he declined to comment on the vote).

profile.background: Alaska House District 8 (northern Kenai Peninsula - Nikiski, Hope, Cooper Landing), Republican, first term (sworn Jan 21 2025; replaced Ben Carpenter). House Republican minority; sits on Resources, Education, and Fisheries. Member, Soldotna Church of the Nazarene; NRA member. Confirmed running for reelection 2026.
profile.confidence_note: First-term member scored from his sponsorship record, four recorded votes, and a cited 2024 iVoterGuide candidate questionnaire (direct self-stated positions = HARD). Broadly conservative with one documented pro-life DEVIATION (HB 195). Denominator is real (15 scored cells).

bill-elam.notes_set: Evidence finalization 2026-06-17. Roster VERIFIED (akleg HD8, R; KDLL 6/11/2026 confirms 2026 reelection bid). HARD conservative via 2024 iVoterGuide + sponsorships: cosponsor of HB 357 'Heartbeat Act'; marriage 'God-ordained union of one man and one woman' and opposes biological males in women's sports/spaces; supports education funding 'following the child' to public/private/homeschool and 'strongly disagrees' with CRT; opposes compelling businesses to violate religious beliefs and says government should never close churches; cosponsor of HB 1 'Specie as Legal Tender' (gold/silver; enacted Ch.6 SLA 26) and opposes ESG criteria for public funds; supports photo voter ID (prime sponsor HB 320 voter registration); opposes defunding police; supports physically securing the southern border; 'no gun-ownership restrictions needed' and supports armed teachers (NRA member). PRO-LIFE DEVIATION: voted YES on HB 195 (House 32-8, 5/15/2026) - one of ~11 Republicans in favor; Alaska Watchman named Elam in the YES column; pro-life groups scored it as abortion-pill expansion - scored FALSE on the abolition cell (counterweighted by his Heartbeat-Act cosponsorship, TRUE on personhood). EDUCATION split (noted, not penalized): voted to SUSTAIN the HB 69 veto but YES to OVERRIDE the HB 57 veto (8/2025). Lone NO on the HB 239 crime omnibus was an explicitly PROCEDURAL objection (bill bundled ~10 proposals) - not an anti-crime position, so not scored against him. PFD-full-statutory, CBDC, RCV-repeal, and a clean 10th-Amendment statement: NO EVIDENCE - null.

0.note: Cosponsor of HB 357 'Alaska Heartbeat Act' (2026) banning abortion after detectable heartbeat - affirms protection of life from early development.

1.note: Voted YES on HB 195 (House 32-8, 5/15/2026), one of ~11 Republicans; pro-life groups (NRLC, Alaska Right to Life, Alaska Watchman, which named Elam) scored it as expanding chemical-abortion access - contrary to abolition.

0.note: States marriage is a 'God-ordained...union of one man and one woman.'

2.note: 'Strongly disagrees' that biological males should participate in women's sports/spaces - affirms biological sex.

0.note: 'Strongly supports' education funding following the child to public, private, or homeschool - supports school choice and homeschool freedom.

2.note: 'Strongly disagrees' with Critical Race Theory in K-12 - opposes CRT curricula.

0.note: Self-identifies as a 'traditional conservative christian' and says government should never close churches - affirms public profession of Christian faith.

1.note: 'Strongly opposes' requiring people/businesses to provide services that violate their religious beliefs - supports conscience exemptions.

0.note: Cosponsor of HB 1 'Specie as Legal Tender' (gold/silver as legal tender, enacted Ch.6 SLA 26) - supports decentralized/sound money, anti-CBDC-adjacent.

4.note: Opposes ESG investment criteria for public funds.

1.note: 'Strongly disagrees' that people should be able to vote without photo ID - supports photo voter ID.

0.note: Opposes redirecting/defunding police toward community programs - publicly backs law enforcement.
'Strongly agrees' the US should do more to physically secure the southern border.

1.note: Believes no gun-ownership restrictions are needed for public safety and supports armed teachers; NRA member - opposes new firearm restrictions.
